headline: School Bullying Reports Reach 14,800 With Cyberbullying Up 120%
context: MOE's anti-bullying unit recorded 14,800 formal bullying reports in 2025 with online harassment cases surging.

cards.big: 14,800 bullying cases reported in Malaysian schools during 2025 academic year
cards.sub: Cyberbullying cases increased 120% from 2023 to 2025 as social media use grows

cards.big: Only 22% of schools have implemented the MOE anti-bullying program since 2019 launch
cards.sub: Program rollout stalled due to lack of training materials and facilitators

cards.big: Bullied students are 2.5 times more likely to drop out before completing SPM
cards.sub: Educational consequences of bullying extend beyond emotional harm to lost achievement

cards.big: Norway's Olweus programme reduced school bullying by 50% over 3 years of implementation
cards.sub: Whole-school approaches with parent involvement show strongest evidence base

cards.big: A school that cannot protect its students from each other cannot claim to educate them
Should schools face consequences for failing to implement mandatory anti-bullying programs
